diagrams.title: Unix System Call Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between user applications, C library, system call interface, and kernel
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how user programs request kernel services through system calls, showing the flow from application to hardware and back

diagrams.title: Process Memory Layout in Unix
diagrams.description: Typical virtual address space layout of a Unix process showing text, data, bss, heap, and stack segments
diagrams.purpose: Shows memory organization of Unix processes and how different segments are used in system programming

diagrams.title: File Descriptor Reference Model
diagrams.description: Shows how file descriptors in a process reference open files through the kernel's internal data structures
elements: System-wide Open File Table (file offset, status flags)
Actual File on Disk or Device
diagrams.purpose: Explains the three-level structure Unix uses to manage open files, essential for understanding file I/O system programming
